LHX.sentence: L3Harris is down slightly, but it's starting to look like decent value compared to its usual trading range — defense spending remains steady.

ASML.sentence: ASML had a small uptick today and continues trading at a premium, which makes sense given they're the only company that makes the advanced chipmaking machines everyone needs.

LIN.sentence: Linde jumped about 3% today — the industrial gas business keeps humming along with its predictable long-term contracts.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft dropped nearly 4%, but Azure cloud revenue is still growing at 33%, which is what really matters for the AI story.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is down about 2% and looking cheaper than usual — when biotech spending eventually picks back up, they're positioned to benefit.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge had a quiet day up slightly — the pipeline business is steady as always, though the dividend yield isn't as attractive as it used to be.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow dropped about 6% but the underlying business of selling AI workflow tools to big companies and government agencies keeps growing.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore jumped nearly 5% today, outperforming other mining stocks — commodity trading profits can be volatile but lucrative.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin dropped 2% and is sitting well below its recent highs, which often sets up decent entry points after big runs.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA was flat today — the Mexican peso exchange rate continues to be the main driver for this Latin American bottler.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras fell about 2%, but with oil prices decent, it's still paying a massive 61% dividend yield this quarter.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk was essentially flat today and trading below the value of its ships and terminals, which provides a decent safety cushion in the shipping business.
